programme.title: Celos y violencia
programme.desc: Salcero se va a bailar salsa. Felipe pide a Guzmán q le de dinero q perdió apuestas. Arriaga le regala a victoria la música q les gustó del radio. Victoria pinta a Arriaga. Kendra saca info de negocios a Nelson.

programme.title: Claustrofobia
programme.desc: Odette visita meta imagen. Victoria no acepta la oferta de Odette. Lili y Niki pelean en el club atlético. Vitoria y Arriaga van a la cita para un nuevo prospecto y quedan atrapados en el elevador.

programme.title: Defensor del abuso
programme.desc: Leo maltrata a Beatriz. Ella abandona su casa. Le pide ayuda a Guzmán. Niki casi descubre a Nelson/kendra. Arriaga/Victoria logran mantener la cita con éxito. Cristina ofrece alojo a Beatriz.

programme.title: Nuevo objetivo
programme.desc: Arriaga desenmascara a Odette. Niki cree q Guzmán defendió a su amante. Tomasina lleva el cheque al papá de Guzmán. Kendra visita a Niki para ir a visitar un diseñador. Tiene un plan para secuestrar a Niki.

programme.title: Nikki secuestrada
programme.desc: Descubren q Niki y Kendra Han sido secuestradas. Salcero quiere dirigir la negociación del secuestro. Piden dinero para dejarla libre. Beatrix quiere trabajar y Cristina ofrece ayuda con Guillo.

programme.title: Culpable del secuestro
programme.desc: Salcero está en contacto con espanto manejando el secuestro. Kendra y salcero conversan q harán con el rescate. Cristina y Aníbal se conocen. Salcero culpa a Guzmán del secuestro.

programme.title: Rescate
programme.desc: Niki atenta escape pero por salvar a Kendra se rinde. Kendra da instrucciones a Espanto de cómo repartirán el botín. Guzmán consigue la dirección de los secuestradores. El y Arriaga van al rescate.

programme.title: Traición y venganza
programme.desc: Salcero previene a Espanto y escapan. Jonas propone a espanto quedarse con el dinero. Arriaga y Guzmán acompañan a Victoria a entregar el rescate.

programme.title: Primer beso
programme.desc: Recuperan a Niki. Kendra feliz se cree rica. Jonás y Espanto traicionan a Kendra. Nelson se va a ver a Kendra. Ella logra q se quede a pasar la noche. Arriaga besa a Victoria mientras ella duerme.

programme.title: Conserjerito inglés
programme.desc: Germán y compañía toman clases de inglés en línea con Silvia. Cuando Miss Silvia reprueba a Germán, este descubre la forma de desquitarse. Rocko tiene problemas de oído y creen que se está quedando sordo.

programme.title: La toalla del mojado 2
programme.desc: Frankie recibe una visita inesperada, la famosa actriz Lyn May, quien lo inspira a que haga la película de La toalla del mojado 2. Pedro se encarga de cuidar a Luisito Jr. para que Luis pueda descansar.

programme.title: La veterinaria de Vanesa
programme.desc: Vanesa por fin cumple el sueño de tener su propia veterinaria y, con ayuda de su novio Fabián, renta el local que está en el edificio. Los López Pérez compran una lavadora nueva y la colocan en el pasillo frente al departamento de Luis.

programme.title: ¡Qué escándalo!
programme.desc: Morris aprende a tocar la batería y los vecinos se quejan por el ruido. Silvia y Luis descubren que ese ruido arrulla al bebé.

programme.title: Arturo influencer
programme.desc: Arturo se vuelve un famoso influencer de TikTok y Paco de Miguel lo busca para una colaboración. Cris recibe la visita de su hermana Esmeralda y Pedro se siente desplazado.

programme.title: El novio de Virginia
programme.desc: Silvia acude a un diplomado que dura dos días y quiere contratar a una niñera, pero Luis no está de acuerdo. Virginia le quiere declarar su amor a un chico y Germán piensa que es él.

programme.title: Bebé a bordo
programme.desc: En el segundo día del diplomado de Silvia, Luisito Jr. queda al cuidado de Pedrito, pero este permite que todos los vecinos lo cuiden y en un descuido pierden al bebé.

programme.title: Atorón
programme.desc: Es el aniversario de Benito y de Liz, pero él no tiene dinero para festejarlo, por lo que ella le pone un ultimátum para continuar con su relación. Luis y Magda discuten porque ambos quieren usar las escaleras.

programme.title: Primera palabra
programme.desc: Liz ha encontrado el lugar perfecto para que ella y Benito vivan juntos pero, ¿Benito dejará la casa de sus padres? El bebé Luisito ha dicho su primera palabra, pero Luis cree que a Silvita no le gustará cuando se entere de qué ha dicho su hijo.

programme.title: El bautizo
programme.desc: Silvita y Luis bautizarán al bebé Luisito. ¿Quiénes serán sus padrinos? Además, ¿qué tendrá que pasar para que Germán sea invitado a este evento? Benito abandona la vecindad por siempre.

programme.title: No se vende
programme.desc: La dueña del edificio falleció y sus dos hijas, 'Maty' y 'Mony' se lo disputan, dejando en medio a los vecinos y a punto de ser desalojados.

programme.title: Regresó Ale
programme.desc: Ale regresa a casa con su familia. Descubre cuál será su reacción al verla de nuevo. Además, Benito gana una audición para una serie.

programme.title: La cisterna y el charro negro
programme.desc: Los vecinos están preocupados porque el agua de la cisterna sale muy sucia.

programme.title: Capitán raquetas
programme.desc: Luis está en problemas, ya que deberá armar un plan para evitar que lo echen de su trabajo. Además, doña Magda se actualiza en la moda y se pone uñas nuevas.

programme.title: Guerra de las albóndigas
programme.desc: Lorena y Liz se pelean para saber quién de las dos prepara las mejores albóndigas enchipotladas. Además, Roco recibe la visita de su madre.

programme.title: Cumple Magda

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.title: MeatEater - Alaska Black Bear with Evan Felker
programme.desc: SRME_1304_AK_BEAR_MASTER.mp4

programme.title: MeatEater - Into the Clouds: Sitka Blacktail Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella charters a float plane to drop off on a remote lake in Alaskas coastal rainforest for a solo.

programme.title: MeatEater - Welcome to Tanzania
programme.desc: Steven Rinella travels to Tanzania for his first African safari with professional hunter, Morgan Potter.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Sweetest Meat: Alaskan Black Bear
programme.desc: As summer turns to fall, Steve heads into the backcountry of Alaska's Chugach Range.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Waters Edge: Waterfowl in Alaska
programme.desc: For a waterfowler's dream hunt, Steven Rinella joins his friend, wildlife biologist Brandt Meixell, on a remote trip to some untouched Alaska wetlands in search of ducks and geese. With a spread of decoys and a loyal retriever, they try to get their limit and fill their bellies with willow-spit roasted whole duck.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Rugged Peaks: Alaskan Mountain Goat
programme.desc: Every year, a handful of hunters draw a mountain goat permit to hunt the Mills Creek region of Alaskas Kenai.

programme.title: MeatEater - The New American Food Chain: Wild Pigs
programme.desc: On this trip, Steve travels to the Sacramento Valley in central California in search of a new challenge.

programme.title: MeatEater - Brotherhood, Badlands, and Pack Llamas: Montana Mule Deer
programme.desc: With a trio of cantankerous, spit-hurling pack llamas, Steven heads out.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Roughest Country: Texas Aoudad Sheep
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads into the mountainous desert backcountry of west Texas after Barbary sheep, or Aoudad. These introduced sheep from North Africa are hard to find and harder to hunt. Challenged by the harsh environment, rugged terrain, and elusive sheep, Steve finds this hunt to be one of the most difficult he has ever undertaken.

programme.title: MeatEater - Canyon Bottoms and Cookouts: Texas Javelina
programme.desc: Steven Rinella takes to the canyons of the West Texas desert in search of javelina. In this broken, rocky terrain finding these unique critters isn't easy and getting a clear shot on a skunk pig with a bow is a whole other story. But Steve is inspired by the evidence of he finds of ancient hunters that used the area to prepare a meal that honors both the animal and our hunting ancestors.

programme.title: MeatEater - Big Bucks and Small Game: Wisconsin Whitetail Deer
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads to Wisconsin to spend a few days on his buddys historical family farm to hunt small game.

programme.title: MeatEater - Big Sky, Bigger Birds: Montana Wild Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his brother Matt load up the llamas for a backcountry spring Turkey hunt in Montana's Powder River Breaks. They will pack deep into the rugged badlands to find gobbling birds. With two tags each, there's plenty of action and camp-fried turkey schnitzel is on the menu.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Fair Chase: Arizona Mountain Lion
programme.desc: In the harsh desert Southwest, Steven Rinella has come to learn the truth about tracking a mountain lions.

programme.title: MeatEater - Crossing Borders: Mexican Bison
programme.desc: Steven Rinella heads into the mountains of the Sonora Desert on a true, wild, free-ranging bison hunt.

programme.title: MeatEater - Strange Hunt in a Strange Land: New Zealand Red Stag
programme.desc: Steven Rinella is on New Zealand's South Island for a do-it-yourself, free-range hunt for the majestic red stag. But first, he needs to secure some camp meat in the form of wild lamb. Battling weather and wary game in mountainous terrain, he uses his spot-and-stalk hunting skills to discover Kiwi red stags are a trophy worthy of the wall and the cook pot.

programme.title: MeatEater - Bush Pig: New Zealand Wild Boar
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and local pig hunting expert, Darren Moore, spend a wild, action-packed day tracking down Captain Cook boars in the thick New Zealand bush. Their tools for the job include two tough little pig dogs, a knife, and some real grit. Wild pigs are dangerous critters and this is as visceral as a hunt gets. Along the way, Steve learns a new technique for field-prepping whole hogs.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Top of the Bottom of the World: New Zealand Thar Part 1
programme.desc: With the help of guide and friend Remi Warren, Steven Rinella gears up for a massive climb into New Zealand's Southern Alps in pursuit of Chamois and Tahr. The hunters will travel light and base out of an alpine hut to find animals Steve has never hunted. Things turns adventurous quickly; with an impassable glacial river between Steve and his goal. But with hard work, Steve experiences a do-it-yourself high country hunt like no other.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Top of the Bottom of the World: New Zealand Thar Part 2
programme.desc: With the help of guide and friend Remi Warren, Steven Rinella gears up for a massive climb into New Zealand's Southern Alps in pursuit of Chamois and Tahr. The hunters will travel light and base out of an alpine hut to find animals Steve has never hunted. Things turns adventurous quickly; with an impassable glacial river between Steve and his goal. But with hard work, Steve experiences a do-it-yourself high country hunt like no other.

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Election Chaos: Texas Republicans vs California Dems)
programme.desc: Could reality tv star Spencer Pratt become Mayor of Los Angeles? And could Trump’s endorsement of Ken Paxton cause Texas to go blue? This week, Alex dives into all things primaries to see if we can read the tea leaves for what awaits both Republicans and Democrats in the future. She’s joined by Nikki Laurenzo, anchor and political reporter for FOX40 in Sacramento, to break down the chaotic and still very undecided races shaping California politics. Then she speaks to Crooked Media’s Dan Pfeifffer, co-host of Pod Save America and author of the Message Box newsletter, to get a read on the consequences of Senator John Cornyn’s loss to Ken Paxton, and why a crowded gubernatorial primary in California could be a warning for Democratic presidential hopefuls in 2028.

programme.title: Hysteria (Welcome to the Jungle Primary w. Reshma Saujani)
programme.desc: Erin and Alyssa cover Trump’s nemesis Thomas Massie’s loss in Kentucky, a positive (for now) Mifepristone update, the ridiculous Garden of Heroes, and what the hell is going on in California politics. Then, Reshma Saujani joins to discuss her new documentary No Country for Mothers, and the real issues moms face. They wrap up in the sanity corner with rave reviews of Cookie Queens and Widow's Bay.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (An In-Studio Spectacular)
programme.desc: Lovett’s getting married, but before he walks down the aisle, he heads into the studio, joining The Bulwark’s Tim Miller to talk ballrooms, breaking news, and Trump’s big, sloppy slush fund. Then Lovett or Leave It writers Halle Kiefer and Sarah Lazarus stop by to share some rehearsal dinner roast jokes, and for a wedding present, they gift Lovett something blue: the coveted Sapphic Token. As his dream foretold! Congrats, boss!

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (How Anxious Should You Be About AI?)
programme.desc: AI development is barreling ahead with no guardrails, leading Americans to feel anxious about what lies ahead. This week, Stacey tackles the big questions we need to ask ourselves about the technology with Katie Drummond, Global Editorial Director at Wired, and Timnit Gebru, founder and Executive Director at the Distributed AI Research Institute (DAIR). They discuss the economic uncertainty created by AI, the motivations driving tech leaders, making sense of the flurry of information and opinions that are influencing the discourse on the issue, and whether we can imagine a more optimistic technologically-forward future.

programme.title: Assembly Required with Stacey Abrams (How SCOTUS Is Erasing Black Voters, and Abby Phillip on Jesse Jackson’s Legacy)
programme.desc: This week, Stacey goes beyond the headlines about the Supreme Court’s recent ruling on the Voting Rights Act and outlines why this step is so dangerous - not just for minority voters around the country - but for our democracy as a whole She’s joined by Lauren Groh-Wargo, CEO of Fair Fight, to discuss what people can still do at the local level and at the ballot box to save our elections and fight back against authoritarianism. Then, Stacey is joined by CNN host Abby Phillip, who talks about the importance of debate in our political culture and what current activists can learn from Jesse Jackson’s legacy of fighting for equality.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Nancy Mace: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 24th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the chaos muppet herself, Nancy Mace. From her freaky obsession with trans people to her desperate attempts to kiss Trump's a**, is Nancy our most attention-seeking guy yet?

programme.title: Runaway Country with Alex Wagner (Ice Goons at the Airport and DACA Detentions (w/ Jonathan V. Last))
programme.desc: Leave it to travel delays during Spring Break to turn America’s focus to immigration again. This week, as Congress debates over funding and reforms to ICE, and Markwayne Mullin is sworn in as new Secretary of the Department of Homeland Security, Alex brings our attention back to the ongoing tactics the Trump administration is using to target immigrants and tear families apart. First she speaks to Stephanie Villarreal, whose husband has been detained despite being a DACA recipient. Then Alex speaks to Jonathan V. Last, editor of The Bulwark, about how to keep a spotlight on immigration, especially when they employ bureaucratic tactics to change someone's status, and why Trump is trying to force a voting rights bill into the debate.

programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) brings his wife, Lisa (Eva Gabor), to their new home, a ramshackle farm near Hooterville, just outside of Pixley, where she gets to meet the town's oddball residents. Bea Benaderet, Edgar Buchanan and Frank Cady guest star!

programme.title: The Decorator
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) is ready to get farming, but Lisa (Eva Gabor) puts her foot down and says she will leave unless they can hire a decorator to fix the house! Meanwhile, Kate Bradley (Bea Benaderet) teaches Lisa how to make "hotscakes."

programme.title: The Best Laid Plans
programme.desc: Is the Honeymoon period over already? The rumor mill has it that Lisa (Eva Gabor) has packed up and left Oliver (Eddie Albert). Actually, she went back to New York to close up their penthouse apartment. And Mr. Haney is up to his old tricks!

programme.title: My Husband The Rooster Renter
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) calls in a roofer, a plumber and agricultural expert Hank Kimball (Alvy Moore). All of them give the same appraisal of his farm: it's a disaster! Meanwhile, Mr. Haney (Pat Buttram) has a few chickens he'd like to rent out!

programme.title: Furniture, Who's Got The Furniture?
programme.desc: The Douglases are awaiting the arrival of their furniture and they don't know it, but Oliver's mother is on her way, too. But when the furniture is accidentally delivered to Mr. Haney's new place, he tries to sell their own possession back to them.

programme.title: Neighborliness
programme.desc: Planting season ends in three weeks and Oliver has yet to get his land plowed. Meanwhile, Uncle Joe (Edgar Buchanan) needs a new shotgun before duck hunting season starts and schemes to solve both of their problems...without Oliver knowing it.

programme.title: Lisa, The Helpmate
programme.desc: When Oliver (Eddie Albert) sends a sample of his soil to the State Scientific College, a report comes back that his farm contains ingredients never before found in soil. Could his soil problems just be cosmetic? Eleanor Audley guest stars.

programme.title: You Can't Plug In A 2 With A 6
programme.desc: Relying on statistics provided by the Department of Agriculture, Oliver (Eddie Albert) decides to plant wheat. But when he goes to Drucker's to buy seed, he learns the other farmers have a unique way to predict the best crop�Mrs. Ziffel's lumbago.

programme.title: Don't Call Us, We'll Call You
programme.desc: After some wheeling and dealing and mending a riff between Mr. Kimball and his mother, Oliver (Eddie Albert) finally has a phone installed, but the Hooterville Phone Company installs it directly outside his bedroom window...on top of the phone pole.

programme.title: Parity Begins At Home
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) plants his entire 160 acres with wheat, only to hear from The Crabwell Corners Conservation and Stabilization Committee, a local farm organization, that he was only permitted to grow eight acres of wheat�because of Mr. Haney.

programme.title: Lisa Has A Calf
programme.desc: The cow that Oliver (Eddie Albert) bought from Mr. Haney is pregnant. Afraid that Haney will claim the new calf, Oliver and Eb decide to keep it secret...only to have the people of Hooterville think that it is Lisa (Eva Gabor) who is expecting!

programme.title: The Wedding Anniversary
programme.desc: It's the Douglases' wedding anniversary and, after initially pretending not to remember, Oliver (Eddie Albert) makes clear that he knows the date very well, he just can't remember exactly how many years he and Lisa (Eva Gabor) have been married.

programme.title: What Happened In Scranton
programme.desc: Lisa (Eva Gabor) is shocked that Hooterville has no beauty parlor, and the Ladies' Every-Other-Wednesday Afternoon-Discussion-Club nominates her to make it happen. Of course, Oliver's mother is happy to help, even if it means underhanded tactics.

programme.title: Give Me Land, Lots Of Land
programme.desc: When Oliver's neighbor, Mr. Watson, decides to do some traveling, Oliver (Eddie Albert) buys the farm which adjoins his property, but not the house on it. Lisa (Eva Gabor) misunderstands and thinks the beautiful farmhouse is their new home.

programme.title: I Didn't Raise My Husband To Be A Fireman
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert) discovers the only requirement to join the Hooterville Valley Fire Department is that he must play a musical instrument. Meanwhile Lisa (Eva Gabor) tries her hand at cooking something besides "hotscakes" for breakfast.

programme.title: Lisa Bakes A Cake
programme.desc: Believing he will never get a moment's peace, Oliver (Eddie Albert) is annoyed when Lisa (Eva Gabor) puts a listing in the new phone book: "Oliver Douglas, Attorney at Law"�but it is a different story when no one calls for his services.

programme.title: The Price Of Apples
programme.desc: Oliver (Eddie Albert)'s apple crop is ready to be harvested, and he decides to take them to market himself and make a killing. Of course, getting to market before the price drops proves a little more difficult than Oliver anticipates.

programme.title: Morticia, The Decorator
programme.desc: Morticia, in order to be part of the National Beautification Program, decides to become a home decorator and offers her services to the neighbors. Next door neighbor, and insurance agent, Joe Digby hires Morticia in hopes of selling them insurance.

programme.title: Ophelia Visits Morticia
programme.desc: Ophelia taunts Uncle Fester into announcing that he is going to join the Peace Corps. Fester feels he's a cinch to be accepted because Gomez plans to pull strings in Washington on his behalf. The entire family agrees to help him study for the exam.

programme.title: Addams Cum Laude
programme.desc: Allyn Joslyn guest-stars as Sam Hilliard, a former truant officer who now heads a private school where Wednesday and Pugsley are enrolled. When Hilliard breaks under the strain of dealing with the Addams children, Gomez buys the school.

programme.title: Cat Addams
programme.desc: Dr. Marvin P. Gunderson is summoned to examine Kitty Kat, the ailing lion pet of the Addams Family. Morticia decides that it is the doctor who needs help. To build his confidence, human members of the family pretend to be ill so he can affect cures.

programme.title: Lurch's Little Helper
programme.desc: Morticia's concern that Lurch is overworked leads Gomez to the operating table to assemble a custom-built secondary butler. Lurch lets the robot do all the work and then begins to feel that he isn't needed anymore.

programme.title: The Addams Policy
programme.desc: When Uncle Fester incinerates the family's stuffed bear with his flamethrower, Morticia reports the accident to the insurance company. Mr. Henson, the head of the company, sends his assistant, Mr. Digby, to handle the case.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Halloween With The Addams Family
programme.desc: A pair of bank robbers are welcomed as Halloween trick-or-treaters by Morticia and Gomez. The creepy atmosphere of the house, Morticia's smoldering holiday punch and Lurch's ominous presence impel the crooks to abandon their plans.

programme.title: Green Eyed Gomez
programme.desc: When Lionel Barker, a former suitor of Morticia, shows up, Gomez is jealous. But he's soon calmed when he learns that Barker is after Mildred, the new maid. The Addamses then discover that Barker is a con man--after Mildred's money, not her love.

programme.title: New Neighbors Meet The Addams Family
programme.desc: Newlyweds move in next door. But the honeymoon loses its tranquility when Uncle Fester enters their house through a tunnel and they flee in terror at the sight of Lurch, Grandmama sharpening her ax, and Morticia and Gomez bathing the octopus.

programme.title: Why Is Everyone So Emotionally Fragile?
programme.desc: Emotional maturity is a difficult thing to truly come by. Making your needs known, setting boundaries, being able to disappoint people without being afraid. If it's such an important skill, why is it so hard to discover how to develop it? Expect to learn how Whitney has been changed since becoming a mother, why Whitney has been thinking about circumcision so much, what codependence is and how to overcome it, why your niceness might be narcissism in disguise, why the news and memes are moving at such an insane velocity right now and much more...

programme.title: The Biology Of Focus, Success & Long-Term Energy
programme.desc: From personal dramas to scientific uproars, it's been a wild year for the biggest health & fitness podcaster in the world. And today we get to discover his biggest new insights about life, relationships and protocols. Expect to learn whether you actually should drink coffee within 90 minutes of waking up, how to get the best sleep of your life according to the latest science, how to become a morning person, what Andrew has learned about the perils of fame and public scrutiny, what new research says on the world of longevity supplements, why you should always do your research when testing with peptides and much more…
